citation.description: PlanSeverityAttrs.ac_unverifiable typed as Literal["none","underspecified_oracle","broken_oracle","missing_oracle"]; tests/unit/test_oracle_grade_split.py lines 57-69 pin acceptance and rejection; both verifier prompt files lines 68-86 define all three grades with examples.

criteria.criterion: The verification model accepts exactly the closed grade set {none, underspecified_oracle, broken_oracle, missing_oracle} for ac_unverifiable on new plan-v3 verifications - pinned by BOTH an acceptance test and a rejection test for an out-of-vocabulary grade - and both verifier prompts (single-turn + agentic) define each grade with one worked example.

citation.description: UNDERSPECIFIED_ORACLE_CONTRIB=0.55, ORACLE_GRADE01 map, ac_unverifiable excluded from both generic _SEV01 loops and special-cased; tests/unit/test_impact_plan.py lines 51-78 pin all three grades and threshold invariant.

criteria.criterion: Impact scoring pinned by unit tests: missing_oracle and broken_oracle findings floor at >= 0.85; underspecified_oracle findings score <= 0.55 (below the 0.60 lowest blocking threshold) and never trigger the hard floor; the other three override axes floor unchanged.

citation.description: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION = 'plan-v3'; test_impact_model_versioning.py line 27 pins it; test_oracle_grade_split.py pins grade persistence (line 100-119), legacy read-as-is (lines 123-146), and enrich asymmetry (lines 91-96); workflow_ops.py lines 118-119 implement the asymmetric clear.

criteria.criterion: IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION is plan-v3 in the same change (test_impact_model_versioning.py updated), the grade persists per finding in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (pinned by test), legacy plan-v2 records are read as-is (segmentation by version is the back-compat mechanism, asserted in the doc note), and enrich_operator_attested clears missing/underspecified but never broken_oracle (pinned by test).

citation.description: Three-grade oracle rule documented with calibration-3 stats (35.5% floor-driven, 48.9% ac_unverifiable, 56% underspecified).
